title: Why didnt 3I occur when Gendo fused with Adam
display_title: Why didnt 3I occur when Gendo fused with Adam
wikitext:
[]: Why didn't 3I occur when Gendo fused with Adam?             Quote Post by nitrogenesis » Sun Jan 03, 2016 1:31 pm [Topic title changed from "Question (spoilers)" for clarity. - Monk] Why didn't third impact occur when Gendo fused his hand with Adam's embryo? Wouldn't Gendo have Lillith DNA in him and the combination set off the explosion? At first I had thought that it was because Adam's soul did not reside within the body, but then after Rei+Adam's body+Lillith's soul returned to Lillith's body I realized that perhaps the soul was not necessary. What did happen to Adam's soul after Kaworu died? Sorry to ask multiple questions.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jan 03, 2016 1:46 pm 1) Third Impact requires Adam and Lilith, specifically, to come together, or a suitable clone of either. A single human or an ordinary Angel isn't really good enough. 2) Adam's soul floated around like all souls probably do, until they were all collected by Giant Naked Rei.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 03, 2016 2:03 pm In any case, the Adam in Gendo's palm thing is an addition in the DC material -- and that whole episode is rather problematic in terms of consistency with itself and its surroundings. See for example thread/2095/The-Great-Episode-24-Plot-Hole-Fanwank-Thread/ post/450417/Filling-the-24-Plot-Hole-Once-and-for-All/ at least.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sun Jan 03, 2016 2:07 pm Wasn't the DC material what was originally in NGE's script, but was left out because of time/budget problems?

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 03, 2016 2:25 pm We have the drafts for episode 24 , and they went in a completely different direction, though. It's possible that some of the material may have been in the settei, but would not have been likely to be in a draft, as even allowing for the padding with static scenes to fill the time allotted, there was more material added in the DC than could fit a broadcast slot (especially to 21 and 22) EoE was certainly closer to what had originally been intended for the last two episodes than what was actually aired, however.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by nitrogenesis » Sun Jan 03, 2016 3:00 pm AuraTwilight wrote: 1) Third Impact requires Adam and Lilith, specifically, to come together, or a suitable clone of either. A single human or an ordinary Angel isn't really good enough.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 03, 2016 10:04 pm Arcadia's legacy#822800 wrote: Wasn't the DC material what was originally in NGE's script, but was left out because of time/budget problems?

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 03, 2016 10:48 pm NemZ wrote: Nope. Most of it was created fresh to tie into EoE.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 03, 2016 11:26 pm No, it's not speculation. We can't know for a fact what was planed for the original 26 but we do know that the plan was greatly elaborated upon before it became EoE, and that the DC stuff was created partially as setup within the Death recap and partly after the movie had already been released. Considering there are scenes in EoE that directly reference scenes that were added for Death/DC this doesn't feel at all like a mere accident of budget cuts.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 03, 2016 11:47 pm NemZ wrote: No, it's not speculation. We can't know for a fact what was planed for the original 26 but we do know that the plan was greatly elaborated upon before it became EoE, and that the DC stuff was created partially as setup within the Death recap and partly after the movie had already been released. Considering there are scenes in EoE that directly reference scenes that were added for Death/DC this doesn't feel at all like a mere accident of budget cuts.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 04, 2016 12:56 am Bagheera wrote: It is speculation, since the fact that this material was all produced at the same time tells us that it might be precisely the result of production issues.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jan 04, 2016 3:16 am NemZ wrote: No. We have statements that 25-26 had to be changed but there is no such information regarding the DC cuts to argue they were always meant to be there. Further, if those weren't always intended then anything built on those scenes was also not originally intended, and thus we can conclude that at least some of EoE was definitely made up later.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jan 04, 2016 6:27 am NemZ wrote: No. We have statements that 25-26 had to be changed but there is no such information regarding the DC cuts to argue they were always meant to be there.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 04, 2016 6:41 am pwhodges wrote: I don't have the arrogance to suggest that the composer should have stopped working out their ideas before they were content to leave them.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Mon Jan 04, 2016 6:48 am NemZ wrote: EoE is much more visually splendid but the narrative it unfolds ruins almost everything that came before it.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jan 04, 2016 11:05 am NemZ wrote: Lack of evidence when it would be expected IS evidence against a claim. It's not definative proof but it is enough to skew what seems likely. Further we know it wasn't there originally and was there later, so the null hypothosis (the one with no burden of proof) is that they are as intended. Claiming otherwise needs evidence, so if you can't point to a cut scene in a script or some interview commenting on why something had to be left out then there is no basis on which to assume it was always meant to be there.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jan 04, 2016 2:08 pm nitrogenesis wrote: So why did the second impact occur then? Lilith didn't come into contact with Adam, a single human did, unless I'm mistaken. Were the requirements for third impact different from those for second?

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 04, 2016 7:26 pm Bagheera wrote: The evidence suggests, and even states outright, that the version of 26 that aired was not what Anno originally had in mind, and that it was a stripped down version of what he intended that amounted to the best he could do while remaining on schedule.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jan 04, 2016 8:17 pm NemZ wrote: On the contrary, 25 is the part that got skipped and the intended psychological ending stretched out to fill 2 episodes. We can infer from the 'real world slippage' bits in EoTV and the dangling plot threads you mentioned that some sort of battle did happen offscreen, and between the original proposal and the mentioning of the eva series that went nowhere we can assume it involved a massive eva-on-eva throwdown. Any particulars beyond that are conjecture.

[]: Re: Question (spoilers)             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 04, 2016 11:43 pm Nobody is arguing otherwise on 25. 26 is the part in question, but after taking the time to look into it I'd forgotten that sources disagree (Yamaga vs. Okada, for example), and I guess if you're going to dismiss and/or read the lines from the RCB in such a tortured fashion then there isn't much point in discussing it yet again.
